question.text: Under SEBI (Merchant Bankers) Regulations, 1992, what is the minimum net worth requirement for a Category II Merchant Banker?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Category II Merchant Bankers must have a minimum net worth of Rs. 10 crore and must generate Rs. 5 crore in revenue over the last three financial years. They are restricted from managing mainboard IPOs. **The Trap:** Rs. 50 crore is the Category I requirement, not Category II. Category II has a lower threshold because they have restricted activities (cannot manage mainboard IPOs). **Cross-Exam Context:** The two-tier structure of merchant banker categories is a relatively recent development. Category I can do everything including mainboard IPOs; Category II cannot manage mainboard IPOs. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Both categories must maintain 25% of their minimum net worth as liquid assets. Merchant bankers handling only debt/hybrid issues are exempt from the revenue requirements.
